How o9 Works
1. Understanding the Basics 2. The Network 3. Data Flow: Demand 4. Data Flow: Supply 5. The Logic 6. The Simulation

Chapter 1

Understanding the Basics

Learn the fundamental building blocks of any supply chain network: the elements that every planning model is built from.

  1. 01

    Items

    The products, materials, and SKUs that flow through your supply chain network.

    3 min
  2. 02

    Transformation Processes (BOM)

    How items are made — the Bill of Materials and the manufacturing logic behind production.

    4 min
  3. 03

    Transportation Processes (BOD)

    How items move between locations — the Bill of Distribution defining your distribution network.

    3 min
  4. 04

    Resources

    The machines, lines, and people whose capacity constrains how much you can produce.

    3 min
  5. 05

    Resource Consumptions

    The link between transformation processes and resources — how much capacity each process uses.

    3 min
  6. 06

    Calendars & Time

    How o9 models time — planning horizons, time buckets, and operational calendars.

    3 min
Start chapter Back to overview
